narrative:
DRIVER
GBP leads H4 CSM at 100 versus a D1 of 8, the largest divergence in the dataset and the single most important short-term signal, with the H4 regime mixed-low and D1 transitioning, driven by copper up 1.0 percent signalling commodity demand rotation while DXY fades to 99.08. GBPJPY at D1-H4 correlation 0.93 with GBPUSD is the cleanest expression, with NZDUSD and NZDJPY forming a reinforcing cluster given NZD H4 CSM at 97. CHF fading from D1 100 to H4 75 means USDCHF is losing its safe-haven bid in real time, contradicting the news theme of USD strength which correlation data overrides.

MOMENTUM
Gaining: None
Fading: AUDJPY H4 momentum 43 falling sharply, D1 rising at 52, TF conflict warns against new D1 longs. CADJPY H4 at 54 dropping fast, D1 at 31, both warn sellers regaining control.

WATCH
Sterling labor market data release hits GBPJPY directly, a weak print confirms D1 sell thesis and reverses the H4 CSM spike. WTI oil down 4.56 percent accelerates CADJPY downside if it breaks 100. Chinese stimulus headlines trigger AUDUSD through the AUDJPY 0.91 correlation cluster, forcing a directional cascade across NZDUSD simultaneously.

themes.theme: US Dollar strength driven by yield support and structural factors
Fed policy uncertainty weighing on long-end yields
Chinese economic weakness and stimulus expectations
Euro downside limited by bearish consensus positioning
Sterling under pressure from political uncertainty and weak labor market
Japanese Yen weakness with intervention threat as floor
Indian Rupee deteriorating on oil price rises
Iran nuclear talks supporting risk-on sentiment and commodity prices
Commodity complex strength on geopolitical and supply risks

key_observation: USD strength persisting despite mixed signals. Long-end yields selling off but structural support remains intact. Safe-haven flows evident with JPY weakness capped by intervention fears. Risk-off mood driving equity selloff alongside inflation concerns. China weakness and UK labor softness adding to growth headwinds.
watch: Iran nuclear deal trajectory and oil price stability. Fed long-end yield dynamics. RBA rate timing signals. Chinese stimulus effectiveness. UK political developments. BoE rate decision implications given IMF guidance.
